query: Uganda Ebola Bundibugyo virus outbreak confirmed case count latest update late June and July 2026. Have any new cases been reported after 21 June 2026 bringing Uganda total above 20? Check ECDC CDTR Week 27, WHO Disease Outbreak News.
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's the bottom line:

**No new Uganda cases have been reported after 21 June 2026.** Uganda's total stands at **20 confirmed cases (2 deaths)** per the latest ECDC update dated 1 July 2026 (data as of 30 June), and has **not risen above 20**. The last confirmed case was 21 June 2026.

Key reconciliation of the sources: WHO DON608 (published 19 June) reported 19 confirmed cases with a last case of 5 June; one further case was confirmed on 21 June, bringing the total to 20, which is reflected in the more recent ECDC update. The ECDC page references CDTR Week 26 as its most recent report — no CDTR Week 27 was found. Meanwhile, the outbreak is expanding rapidly in the DRC (1,333 cases, ~399 deaths as of 29–30 June), not in Uganda.

sources.title: Ebola disease outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda
sources.snippet: Last updated 1 July 2026 17:40. Uganda: 20 confirmed cases including two deaths; last confirmed case 21 June, no new cases since. DRC: 1,333 confirmed cases as of 29 June.

sources.title: Ebola disease caused by Bundibugyo virus, DRC & Uganda (DON608)
sources.snippet: Published 19 June 2026. As of 18 June, Uganda reported 19 confirmed cases including two deaths, plus one probable case who died. Last confirmed case identified 5 June 2026 at time of publication.

sources.title: 2026 Ebola epidemic
sources.snippet: 438 confirmed deaths in DRC and two deaths in Uganda as of 30 June. Caused by Bundibugyo ebolavirus; PHEIC declared by WHO on 16 May 2026.

sources.title: Ebola Outbreak Tracker — 2026 Bundibugyo Strain
sources.snippet: Uganda 20c / 2d · 1 child positive · BORDER CLOSED. DRC 1,307c / 377d. France 1 confirmed. Germany recovered/discharged. USA (Milwaukee) 3 monitoring.

sources.title: Ebola disease caused by Bundibugyo virus, DRC & Uganda (DON605)
sources.snippet: 134 confirmed cases (nine in Uganda) including 18 deaths reported around 21 May 2026.
